title: Strategic SEO: A Quick Start to Reach a Wider Audience
description:
Ready to demystify SEO and reach a wider audience—without getting overwhelmed? In this fast-paced and approachable course, illustrator, educator, and creative entrepreneur Peggy Dean gives you a no-fluff, quick-start guide to strategic SEO that anyone can follow.

This course is perfect for small business owners, bloggers, creatives, and anyone building an online presence who wants to increase visibility without diving into overly technical jargon or time-consuming strategies.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:

Understand the foundations of on-page and off-page SEO

Apply easy, practical techniques to boost your search rankings

Optimize your content and website without needing prior experience

Use social media and broken link strategies to support your SEO goals

Monitor your progress with beginner-friendly tools

To succeed in this course, no background in SEO or marketing is required—just a willingness to try simple, focused strategies that get results.

With Peggy’s signature down-to-earth style, you'll walk away empowered and equipped to grow your audience, one smart SEO move at a time.

Instructor's Bio:
Peggy Dean is an artist, educator, and best-selling author with a passion for sparking creativity in others. Native to the Pacific Northwest, Peggy has explored nearly every artistic avenue—from ballet and fire dancing to calligraphy, hand lettering, painting, illustration, and beyond. Her curiosity and love for learning fuel her teaching, where she shares approachable techniques to help others find joy in the creative process.

Peggy is the founder of The Pigeon Letters and the author of the best-selling books Nature Drawing & Watercolor, The Ultimate Brush Lettering Guide, and Botanical Line Drawing. Through her classes, blog, and social platforms, she continues to inspire a global community of artists and makers to embrace their own unique style and creative journey.

Find daily inspiration on Instagram @thepigeonletters
 or visit her blog for curated tutorials and resources.
